{{Quote|The Hidden Daggers were a minor band of highly skilled Assassins founded by a disavowed Flail Cutthroat Lieutenant. They were never well known, taking only small bounties and have since degenerated into a band of thugs and pirates.|[[Prisk Kith'Vys]], to a pilot|<ref name="SWG:PK" />}}
The '''Hidden Daggers''' were a [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] organization active in the [[Corellian system/Legends|Corellian system]] at the time of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].

==Description==
The Hidden Daggers were based in the [[Binayre Razorcat]] of the Corellian system.<ref name="TCSWE" /> They maintained a strong presence in the Binayre Razorcat quadrant and in [[The Void (Corellian system)|The Void]].<ref name="SWG:JtL" /><ref name="Sony" /> Members of the organization were also sometimes seen around major [[City/Legends|cities]] on [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]].<ref name="SWG" />

In space battles, the Hidden Daggers flew [[M3-A Scyk interceptor/Legends|M3-A Scyk fighters]], [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighters]], [[G1-M4-C Dunelizard fighter]]s, [[RZ-1 A-wing interceptor/Legends|RZ-1 A-wing interceptors]], [[Rihkxyrk assault fighter]]s, [[Firespray-31-class patrol and attack craft/Legends|''Firespray-31''-class starships]],<ref name="SWG:JtL" /> [[BTL-S3 Y-wing starfighter/Legends|BTL-S3 Y-wing starfighters]], [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|YT-1300 freighters]], [[Ye-4 gunship]]s and [[Warlord Heavy Cruiser]]s. One such cruiser was the ''[[Daggers Point]]''.<ref name="SWG:HDPQP">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=collection|Hidden Daggers Pilot Quota Progress}}</ref>

==History==
The Hidden Daggers were founded by a disavowed [[The Flail|Flail]] [[Assassin/Legends|cutthroat]] [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]].<ref name="SWG:PK" /> Hence, they then entered in a permanent conflict with the Flail.<ref name="FS">{{SonyCite|url=content.jsp?page=Faction%20Standings|text=Faction Standings}}</ref> They were originally a band of highly skilled assassins but they remained quite secretive, taking only small bounties, and never gained strong notoriety.<ref name="SWG:PK" />

[[File:HiddenDaggers-ChampionsOfTheForce.png|thumb|left|200px|A member of the Hidden Daggers.]]
The Hidden Daggers later degenerated into a band of thugs and pirates<ref name="SWG:PK" /> who attacked and plundered defenseless starships in the Corellian system, especially in the lawless Binayre Razorcat quadrant.<ref name="SWG:JtL" /><ref name="Sony" /> The pirates therefore became a target for the [[Corellian Security Force]] ans for their allies of [[Beldonna's League]]. However, they were supported by the [[Rogue CorSec]] criminals.<ref name="FS" /> At some point early in their existence, the Hidden Daggers also entered in conflict with the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutts]] and fought constantly for the control of The Void in the Corellian system. By the time of the Galactic Civil War, the reasons of the conflict had been forgotten.<ref name="SWG:JtL" /><ref name="Sony" />

After the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], the Hidden Daggers extented their operation to other [[Star system/Legends|star system]].<ref name="SWG:C8" /> They also established a permanent presence in the restricted [[Dathomir system/Legends|Dathomir system]]. Some of their [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] were even spotted near an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Space station/Legends|space station]].<ref name="SWG:JtL" /><ref name="Sony_Dathomir">{{SonyCite|url=en_US/players/content.vm?id=66852&resource=features|text=Star Chart: Dathomir System}}</ref> Around that time, they co-operated with [[Galactic Vacant Building Demolitions]], demolishing abandoned buildings using aerial bombardment. These operations served as a basis for the creation of the [[Galactic Vacant Building Demolishing Movement]] by the Empire.<ref name="MIoLI">{{SonyCite|url=en_US/players/content.vm?id=66958|text="Move It or Lose It"|int=Move It or Lose It}}</ref>

In [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]],<ref name="SWG_timeline">{{SWG_timeline}}</ref> the Hidden Daggers obtained sensitive information about secret operations of the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] special unit [[Black Epsilon]] on Corellia. The pirates planned to deliver the data to the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] but the ship carrying the data, the ''[[Torton Runner]]'', was intercepted in the Corellian system by Imperial starfighters led by [[Prisk Kith'Vys]]. The Empire later destroyed the ship with its crew and left evidences that the Rebels were responsible for the attack.<ref name="SWG:CtTR">{{SWGcite|exp=JtL|type=space|Capture the Torton Runner|Corellian system}}</ref>

In the same [[Standard year/Legends|year]], CorSec [[Sergeant/Legends|Sergeant]] [[Rhea]] ordered an attack on several leaders of the Hidden Daggers to combat the increasing pirate activity in the Corellian system. A skilled CorSec [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] managed to engage and kill the [[Hidden Daggers Eliminator]]<ref name="SWG:DtHDE">{{SWGcite|exp=JtL|type=space|Destroy the Hidden Dagger's Eliminator|Corellian system}}</ref> and the Hidden Dagger [[Captain/Legends|captains]] [[Russ Grimblade]], [[Xopw Laow]], [[Mori]] the Bastard, [[Owpa Awoz]] and [[Quixio]] the Rotten.<ref name="SWG:SabttHDL" /> Around the same time, an unit of [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[Rebel pilot/Legends|pilots]] commanded by Rebel [[Officer/Legends|Officer]] [[Acun Solari]] attacked and destroyed a group of Hidden Dagger starfighters in the Corellian system.<ref name="SWG:E">{{SWGcite|exp=JtL|type=faction|Rebel|Enough!!|Corellia}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The Hidden Daggers appeared in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]'', a [[Wikipedia:Massively multiplayer online role-playing game|massively multiplayer online roleplaying game]] developed by [[Sony]] Online Entertainment and published by [[LucasArts]],<ref name="SWG" /> prior to its closure on [[December 15]], [[2011]].<ref name="Shutdown">{{SonyCite|url=playerinfo|text=IMPORTANT INFORMATION ABOUT ''STAR WARS'' GALAXIES™}}</ref>

The Hidden Daggers were one of various thug groups that coud be found in Corellian cities in ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]''. With the addition of space sectors in the first expansion, [[Star Wars Galaxies: Jump to Lightspeed|''Jump to Lightspeed'']], Hidden Daggers starfighters were added to the Corellian and Dathomir systems. They were also featured in several CorSec and Imperial pilot missions.<ref name="SWG:JtL" /> With the release of the "[[W:c:swg:Chapter 8 Notes for Jan 15 2008|Chapter 8]]," on [[January 15]], [[2008]],<ref name="C8">{{SonyForumsCite|url=swg/posts/list.m?topic_id=577070|text=Chapter 8: The Nova Orion Crisis}}</ref> various space-based kill collections were added to the game and additional Hidden Daggers starfighters, transports and gunships were added to other star systems.<ref name="SWG:C8" /> Eventually, the "[[W:c:swg:Game Update 16|Game Update 16]]," released on [[March 23]], [[2010]],<ref name="SonyCite-Game Update 16: ''Star Wars'' Galaxies™: Stronghold of the Lok Revenants">{{SonyCite|url=players/news_archive.vm?id=68713&month=032010|text=Game Update 16: ''Star Wars'' Galaxies™: Stronghold of the Lok Revenants}}</ref> allowed players to fight unlimited waves of Hidden Daggers vessels spawned from the [[Dathomir Station|Dathomir]], [[Lok Station|Lok]] and [[Tatooine Space Station|Tatooine space stations]].<ref name="SWG:GU16" />

The group was also mentioned in "[[Move It or Lose It]]" in [[2007]], a small [[in-universe]] introduction to the first [[W:c:swg:House Demolition Program|House Pack-Up]] event in ''Star Wars Galaxies''.<ref name="MIoLI" />
